channel.title: kt.academy
channel.description: Best articles about programming and best practices.

item.title: How to correctly implement Pull-to-refresh on mobile
item.description: One of the most important pattern on mobile, and how to implement it.

item.title: Understand Compose in 3 minutes
item.description: How Compose actually works? How it works under the hood and why it's so powerful.

item.title: LLVM: The tool that shaped modern programming and no one talks about
item.description: What is LLVM and how it revolutionized the way we write and compile code.

item.title: Why phenix is the logo of kt.academy?
item.description: Fascinating symbology behind the kt.academy logo.

item.title: Hedy Lamarr: Famous actress and mother of Bluetooth?
item.description: Fascinating story of Hedy Lamarr, the Hollywood star who is considered the mother of Bluetooth.

item.title: The future of mobile development
item.description: What the future holds for mobile development, and how it changed in recent years.

item.title: Kotlin in 3 minutes
item.description: The essence of Kotlin, what is it and why it gets so popular.

item.title: What are cohort courses?
item.description: The evolution of learning formats, from books, workshops to cohort courses.

item.title: New big challenger in UI development
item.description: How Compose is changing the game in UI development.

item.title: Collection Literals in Kotlin
item.description: The new feature in Kotlin that allows for concise and expressive collection creation.

item.title: Why do developers count from zero?
item.description: Why do developers count from zero? Short history and reasoning behind this convention.

item.title: In what order are Compose modifiers applied?
item.description: How Compose modifiers are applied and why their order matters.

item.title: Structured Concurrency
item.description: The only guide to structured concurrency you'll ever need.

item.title: Is Kotlin a savior of Java?
item.description: How Kotlin woke Java up, and what it means for the future of Java development.

item.title: IntelliJ's New Kotlin Coroutine Inspections, Explained
item.description: Exploration and explanation of new Kotlin coroutine inspections in IntelliJ IDEA.

item.title: Flow Guessing Game!
item.description: A fun game that pushes your understanding of Kotlin Flow.

item.title: From Python to Kotlin: A transition worth making
item.description: Exploring the benefits of switching from Python to Kotlin for modern backend development.

item.title: Quiz: Do you understand recomposition and stability?
item.description: Do you understand recomposition and stability? Finish this quiz to find out!

item.title: Simplest Nav2 to Nav3 Migration Guide
item.description: How to migrate routes, navigation calls, and transitions from Nav2 to Nav3.

item.title: You don't understand recomposition
item.description: Clear explanation of how composition and recomposition work in Jetpack Compose.

item.title: Top Projects for Coroutines Mastery in 2025
item.description: Overview of the best projects for Coroutines Mastery course in 2025

item.title: You don't understand stability in Compose
item.description: Deep dive into how Compose handles stability and why it's crucial for developers.

item.title: In the world of AI, companies will need MORE developers, not fewer
item.description: Why the rise of AI means an increased demand for skilled developers.

item.title: The power of exercises: How to design them well
item.description: What are the main priorities when designing good exercises for a course or a book.

item.title: Minimal setup for Compose Desktop
item.description: How to setup Compose to use it on desktop, for providing UI for scripts or a simple project.

item.title: Suspending functions vs. Flow: When to use which?
item.description: A comprehensive guide to understanding the differences between suspending functions and Flow in Kotlin Coroutines, and when to use each.

item.title: How to implement a random coroutines challenge generator
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at how to implement a generator of interesting challenges for learning Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Discussing Kotlin Coroutines on Talking Kotlin Podcast
item.description: An insightful discussion about Kotlin Coroutines on the Talking Kotlin podcast.

item.title: Coroutines vs Reactor: When Elegance and Simplicity Crush Complexity
item.description: A comparison of Kotlin Coroutines and Reactor, highlighting the elegance and simplicity of Coroutines over the complexity of Reactor.

item.title: Suspending functions into blocking functions or CompletableFuture
item.description: A guide on how to correctly convert suspending functions into blocking functions or CompletableFuture in Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Kotlin Flow to RxJava or Reactor and vice versa
item.description: A guide on how to convert Kotlin Flow to RxJava or Reactor streams and vice versa.

item.title: Kotlin Coroutines and Swift
item.description: How to use Kotlin Coroutines in Swift projects, or Swift libraries from Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Kotlin Coroutines and JavaScript
item.description: How to use Kotlin Coroutines in JavaScript projects, or JavaScript libraries from Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: runBlocking in practice: Where it should be used and where not
item.description: A comprehensive guide to using runBlocking in Kotlin Coroutines, including best practices and common pitfalls.

item.title: New Cohort of Kotlin Coroutines Experts
item.description: The grand goal of the Coroutines Mastery course.

item.title: Flow from suspending functions and vice versa
item.description: A guide on how to convert suspending functions to Flow and vice versa in Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Suspending functions or flows into callbacks
item.description: A guide on how to correctly convert suspending functions or flows into callback-based functions in Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Building Your First Kotlin Multiplatform App: From Setup to iOS App Store with Compose Multiplatform (2025 Guide)
item.description: A comprehensive guide to building your first Kotlin Multiplatform app using Compose Multiplatform, covering setup, development, and deployment

item.title: Calling blocking functions from suspending functions
item.description: A guide on how to correctly convert blocking functions into suspending functions in Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: How to turn callback functions into suspend functions or Flow
item.description: A guide on how to correctly convert callback-based functions into suspending functions or Flows in Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: KotlinConf 2025 Recordings
item.description: My list of must-watch from KotlinConf 2025.

item.title: Coroutines Races Guessing Game!
item.description: A fun game that pushes your understanding of Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: KotlinConf 2025 Announcements
item.description: The greatest announcements from KotlinConf 2025.

item.title: Coroutines Mastery
item.description: 5-weeks-long kohort workshop to master Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Benefit: Open Workshop Recording
item.description: Introduction of the new benefit for open workshops attendees.

item.title: Kotlin Compiler Plugins
item.description: All you need to know about Kotlin Compiler plugins.

item.title: The problems of Kotlin/Wasm I had when making a game
item.description: A short story of the problems I needed to face when publishing a simple game made with Kotlin/Wasm.

item.title: Collection Processing Guessing Game!
item.description: A fun game that pushes your understanding of Kotlin collection processing.

item.title: How modifiers order affects Compose UI appearance
item.description: A complete explanation of how modifier order affects the appearance of your Jetpack Compose UI.

item.title: Kotlin Christmas Dream
item.description: My dream about the future of Kotlin.

item.title: Is reflection slowing down your code?
item.description: Let's benchmark reflection and see how it affects the performance of your code.

item.title: Jetpack Compose Modifier Guessing Game!
item.description: A fun game to learn about Jetpack Compose modifiers.

item.title: Using BlockHound to track blocking calls in non-blocking dispatchers
item.description: How to use BlockHound to track blocking calls in non-blocking dispatchers.

item.title: Representing ViewModel events with StateFlow vs. SharedFlow vs. Channel
item.description: What is the best way to represent ViewModel events in Kotlin?

item.title: The best dispatcher for a backend framework
item.description: Let's explore different dispatchers and find the best one for a backend request handlers.

item.title: Win a Free Ticket to Droidcon Italy!
item.description: Upgrade your Kotlin skills and win a free ticket to Droidcon Italy!

item.title: Books for 2025 company workshops attendees promotion
item.description: Our special promotion for company workshops organised in 2025.

item.title: Why using Kotlin Coroutines?
item.description: The explanation of why coroutines stand out and offer us what hasn't been offered by other technologies.

item.title: Which Kotlin workshop to choose?
item.description: A short guide how to choose the best workshop for you.

item.title: Item 31: Respect abstraction contracts
item.description: Just because we can do something, does not mean we should. How to respect abstraction contracts and why it is so important.

item.title: Job and children awaiting in Kotlin Coroutines
item.description: What Job is and how it is the most important context responsible for structured concurrency.

item.title: Finance your workshop by company
item.description: How to get your workshop financed by your company.

item.title: Item 30: Define contracts with documentation
item.description: How do we express our library or module contracts and why are they so important.

item.title: Kotlin Coroutines dispatchers
item.description: Where we should use each dispatcher from the Kotlin Coroutines library.

item.title: SharedFlow vs StateFlow
item.description: When to use SharedFlow and when to use StateFlow.

item.title: Item 27: Specify API stability
item.description: How do we specify API stability and why it is so important.

item.title: Power Assert now in Kotlin!
item.description: What is Power Assert, how to use it, and how it works in Kotlin.

item.title: The problem of union types for type systems
item.description: Why union types are not such a good idea for static type systems.

item.title: Key advantages of Kotlin Coroutines
item.description: Where Kotlin Coroutines shine and why you should use them.

item.title: Cancellation in Kotlin Coroutines
item.description: Everything you need to know about the cancellation mechanism in Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Mutable objects or properties?
item.description: Time to answer the question: var/readonly vs val/mutable. Which one should you use?

item.title: Learn Kotlin with Marcin Moskała
item.description: See what materials and options from Marcin Moskala you can have to learn Kotlin.

item.title: The third edition of Kotlin Coroutines: Deep Dive book is finally ready!
item.description: Learn about the new release of the famous book about Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Updates in Kotlin for Developers series
item.description: Noticed any changes in our Kotlin for Developers series? Here's what's new!

item.title: How many threads does your network client use?
item.description: Many popular network clients consume way more threads than you might expect. Let's overview the problem and find a solution.

item.description: A collection of Kotlin bites of knowledge, in a form of tweets.

item.description: Functional Kotlin was updated! Now it has exercises to help readers practice the knowledge.

item.description: Why knowledge repetition is so problematic and how it relates to the single responsibility principle.

item.description: Using programming to simulate a battle in Risc, and learn about its fairness.

item.description: Do you want to learn Kotlin? Join our Big Kotlin Challenge!

item.description: How to interoperate between Kotlin and JavaScript.

item.description: Implement your first annotation processor in Kotlin.

item.description: Using non-standard collection types to improve performance in Kotlin.

item.description: All the essential aspects of KSP in practice.

item.title: Data classes in Kotlin
item.description: What are data classes in Kotlin and how do we use them.

item.description: Why we should use object declarations instead of regular classes.

item.description: Using type references, with practical example of random value generator.

item.description: All the books from the series Kotlin for Developers are ready for Kotlin 2.0!

item.title: Implementing Multiplatform Kotlin Mobile
item.description: How in Kotlin we can implement Android and iOS projects with shared logic.

item.description: How in Kotlin we can use multiplatform capabilities to distribute the same code to multiple platforms.

item.description: The essence of using multiplatform Kotlin capabilities and definition common elements.

item.description: The story behind the lighthouse used on the new edition of Effective Kotlin.

item.description: Learn about the new release of the famous book about the best practices for Kotlin development.

item.title: Sealed classes and interfaces in Kotlin
item.description: What are sealed classes and interfaces in Kotlin and how do we use them.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 2: Eliminate critical sections
item.description: Learn why do we need to synchronize access to mutable state, and how to secure it.

item.title: Kotlin and Java interoperability: Traps and gotchas
item.description: The parts of Kotlin and Java interoperability that might be surprising or counterintuitive.

item.title: Kotlin and Java interoperability: Useful annotations
item.description: Learn about useful Kotlin annotations, that decide that it is compiled to on JVM.

item.title: Kotlin and Java interoperability: Properties and annotations
item.description: Kotlin property is Java getter, setter, field and possibly more. So what to do, when you need to annotate or use a specific JVM element?

item.title: Kotlin and Java interoperability: Types
item.description: See surprising transitions that happens on types when you interoperate between Kotlin and Java.

item.description: Learn about our recently published book dedicated to the advanced Kotlin features.

item.title: Objects in Kotlin
item.description: What is object expression, object declaration and companion objects, and how we use them.

item.title: Kotlin Contracts
item.description: Practical review of Kotlin Contracts - a feature we often use, without knowing about it.

item.title: Eliminating coroutine races
item.description: How to make one coroutine await for another coroutine or flow subscription.

item.title: Lindy effect in surnames problem
item.description: Using programming to simulate a problem of the number of surnames in population and observing Lindy effect.

item.title: The beauty of Kotlin type system
item.description: How type system makes Kotlin so flexible, safe, and intuitive.

item.title: Understanding Flow
item.description: We will explore how flow and its processing really works.

item.description: Learn about the new release of the famous book about Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Why you should consider migrating your Gradle scripts to Kotlin DSL
item.description: Discover the benefits of migrating your Gradle scripts to Kotlin DSL and streamline your development process.

item.description: How do we use Map as a property delegate, and why the result of a famous puzzler should not be surprising.

item.description: What are observable and vetoable delegates, and what are their real-life use cases.

item.description: Let's review the Kotlin Coroutines best practices.

item.title: Kotlin revolutionary announcement
item.description: Kotlin announcement that will change the way we all will write code.

item.title: Lazy property delegate
item.description: What is lazy delegate, and what are its real-life use cases.

item.title: Property delegation
item.description: What is property delegation, how does it work, and how can we implement our custom property delegate.

item.title: Nullability in Kotlin
item.description: How Kotlin null-safety works, and how can we deal with nullable values.

item.title: Interface Delegation
item.description: One of the least known Kotlin features, that can be sometimes found really useful.

item.description: A collection of Kotlin bites of knowledge, in a form of tweets.

item.title: Basic values in Kotlin
item.description: Learn about the basic Kotlin values, types and operations.

item.title: Variance modifiers limitations
item.description: What limitations generic variance modifiers introduce, and how can we ignore them.

item.title: Covariant Nothing Object
item.description: The pattern we are all using, but no-one talks about it.

item.title: Kotlin Generic Variance Modifiers
item.description: What are out and in, and how do we use them without even knowing.

item.title: Enum classes in Kotlin
item.description: What are enum classes in Kotlin and how do we use them.

item.title: Operator overloading in Kotlin
item.description: How are operators defined for types in Kotlin, and how can we define our own operators.

item.description: Learn about the amazing capabilities of Kotlin for-loop.

item.title: Kotlin bites on Twitter 1
item.description: A collection of Kotlin bites of knowledge, in a form of tweets.

item.title: Your first program in Kotlin
item.description: How to write your first program, how can we use it in real-life projects, and how to explore what Kotlin is compiled to.

item.title: Running Kotlin coroutines on Project Loom's virtual threads
item.description: How to use Project Loom to improve Coroutines performance.

item.title: A birds-eye view of Arrow: Data Immutability with Arrow Optics
item.description: Learn to use Arrow Optics to support working on immutable objects.

item.title: Kotlin Essentials book is finally ready!
item.description: Learn about our recently published book dedicated to the essential Kotlin features.

item.title: Functions in Kotlin
item.description: Learn about what functions can offer us in Kotlin.

item.title: Slowing down your code with Coroutines
item.description: How can we use coroutines to slow down our code, and why would we want to do that.

item.title: Kotlin Coroutines use cases introduction
item.description: Introducing a series of articles about common Kotlin Coroutines use cases.

item.title: A birds-eye view of Arrow: Error Handling
item.description: Learn about Error Handling in Arrow, end how to effectively work with Either.

item.title: Kotlin Coroutines use cases for Presentation/API/UI Layer
item.description: How do we use Kotlin Coroutines in the Presentation/API/UI Layer Layer, how do we create scope and start coroutines.

item.title: A birds-eye view of Arrow: working with function with Arrow Core
item.description: Learn about essential Arrow functions used for composition, memoization, recursion and functions generation.

item.title: Kotlin Coroutines use cases for Domain Layer
item.description: How do we use Kotlin Coroutines in the Domain Layer.

item.title: Kotlin Coroutines use cases for Data/Adapters Layer
item.description: How do we use Kotlin Coroutines in the Data/Adapters Layer, how do we use callback or blocking functions.

item.title: Functional Kotlin book is finally ready!
item.description: Learn about our recently published book dedicated to functional Kotlin features.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 10: Design for readability
item.description: Do we really want out applications concise, or do we rather want them readable?

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 7: Prefer a nullable or Result result type when the lack of a result is possible
item.description: Why should we prefer to avoid throwing exceptions and using types to our advantage.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 5: Specify your expectations for arguments and state
item.description: How do we specify requirements and expectations in Kotlin.

item.title: Context receivers
item.description: All you need to know about the new and powerful Kotlin feature: context receivers.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 1: Limit mutability
item.description: Why it is so important to limit mutability, and how Kotlin supports it.

item.title: Scope functions
item.description: What is let, also, apply and with, and how to use them in our applications.

item.title: Type modelling in Kotlin
item.description: Type safety is undervalued in most software projects and causes enormous financial losses throughout the world. Changing a few bad habits can lead to more reliable software.

item.title: DSL type-safe builders
item.description: How to define type-safe DSL builders in Kotlin.

item.title: Lambda expressions
item.description: Let's learn how to use lambda expressions, what special support they have, and what traps they generate.

item.title: Object-oriented or functional? Two ways to see the world
item.description: The difference between the OOP and FP paradigms is deeply rooted in how we see the world. A few words on the philosophy of both approaches.

item.title: Essential programming nomenclature
item.description: Find out what you need to know, before you start writing documentations, books or articles about programming.

item.title: Function references
item.description: How do we reference functions in Kotlin, to replace lambda expressions. All functions, including constructors, getters, extensions and more.

item.title: Best Principles for Successful Test Automation (API and UI)
item.description: Aligning our testing activities with ISTQB standards helps us to perform efficiently and cope with project scope and deadlines. In this workshop we will go over software testing stages starting from definition to maintenance.

item.title: Leveraging AI in Software Testing
item.description: As AI is confronting us in our daily lives and it is improving the development activities by eliminating some manual efforts, it is beneficial to embrace in our tasks. We will see how we can utilize AI based algorithms in Software Testing.

item.title: Software Testing Standards (Aligned with ISTQB)
item.description: Aligning our testing activities with ISTQB standards helps us to perform efficiently and cope with project scope and deadlines. In this workshop we will go over software testing stages starting from definition to maintenance.

item.title: How to write a technical book?
item.description: A guide for writing technical books.

item.title: Persistent memory design choices and use-cases
item.description: Let's understand some design choices with persistent memory and use-cases of persistent memory.

item.title: Why is Kotlin your next Programming Language?
item.description: What is Kotlin, and why it should be your next programming language.

item.title: Persistent dictionary
item.description: Let's make our in-memory dictionary persistent and understand some concepts of PMDK.

item.title: Code or No-code?
item.description: What are the strong sides of code and no-code, and why do they constantly fight with each other.

item.title: Introducing persistent memory
item.description: Let's learn the basics of persistent memory, its characteristics, how it works and the beauty of byte addressability.

item.title: In-memory dictionary
item.description: Let's begin our journey to understand persistent memory by building an in-memory dictionary.

item.title: Persistent memory - Introduction
item.description: Do you want to learn about persistent memory? Join this journey to explore persistent memory and build a persistent dictionary.

item.description: A collection of funny comics for programmers.

item.description: Learn about our recently published book dedicated to Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: SharedFlow and StateFlow
item.description: Known as a replacement for Subject, LiveData, and many more. SharedFlow and StateFlow are powerful coroutines classes, every Kotlin developer should know.

item.description: A basic feature, that is commonly misunderstood.

item.description: How we test Kotlin Coroutines, including common Android and backend cases.

item.description: How we generally define coroutine scope on Android and on the backend.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 56: Consider using groupingBy instead of groupBy
item.description: What Grouping is, and how groupingBy instead of groupBy can be a performance optimization.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 55: Consider associating elements to a map
item.description: How associateBy is useful to improve the performance of finding elements.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 54: Prefer Sequences for big collections with more than one processing step
item.description: What the difference between list and sequence processing is, and when each should be preferred.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 53: Eliminate obsolete object references
item.description: How to help our garbage collector and avoid memory leaks.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 52: Consider using inline value classes
item.description: What value classes are, how to use and inline them.

item.title: Coroutines under the hood
item.description: A deep dive into how suspension and continuations work under the hood.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 51: Use the inline modifier for functions with parameters of functional types
item.description: How inline functions work and why they can be so important for the performance of our application.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 47: Avoid unnecessary object creation
item.description: About the most essential rule of performance optimization.

item.title: Traits for testing in Kotlin
item.description: A trick needed when using traits for testing in Kotlin.

item.title: How does suspension work in Kotlin coroutines?
item.description: A deep explanation of how suspension works in Kotlin Coroutines.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 32: Consider factory functions instead of secondary constructors
item.description: What factory functions are and why they are so important patterns for object creation.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 46: Avoid member extensions
item.description: What member extensions are, how they are possible and why we should avoid using them.

item.title: kt.academy new logo: Phoenix
item.description: Why we decided to change our logo, and what is the symbolism of Phoenix.

item.title: Effective Kotlin Item 36: Prefer composition over inheritance
item.description: Years of OOP made us overuse inheritance. Instead, we should more often use a composition that is safer and more explicit. More often, but not always...
